No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (4.3) and aims to develop independent research ability and deepen professional knowledge in constitutional law, including fundamental rights (4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: The study of 'fundamental rights' (基本權) provides a foundational legal framework for non-discrimination and enforceable legislation for gender equality (5.c).

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The study of 'fundamental rights' (基本權) can tenuously include labor rights, relevant to protecting workers (8.8), though not a primary focus.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's focus on 'constitutional review' (違憲審查) and 'fundamental rights' (基本權) directly addresses eliminating discriminatory laws and reducing inequalities (10.3).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to climate change or action.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course fundamentally focuses on 'constitutional litigation' (憲法訴訟制度), promoting the rule of law, access to justice (16.3), and protecting fundamental freedoms (16.10).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course aims to expand understanding of 'international constitutional development trends' and foster 'global vision' (Core Competency D), aligning with policy coherence (17.14).
